sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Mithril Protocol continuous development through the Intersect budget process
Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, administered by Intersect for the vendor
Deliverables: Mithril Protocol Continuous Development, administrative oversight through Intersect budget administration, milestone-based disbursement controls via TRSC and PSSC smart contracts

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 3,810,423 ada, consisting of 3,699,440 ada for Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ada for an Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Mithril is described as using stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way so nodes and applications can verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Mithril addresses slow full-node synchronization and centralized-trust dependence by improving secure access to and verification of blockchain state.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that funding Mithril through Intersect MBO supports Cardano scaling while preserving decentralization guarantees and reducing dependency on centralized indexers and hosted APIs.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal claims it achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and references external voting and audit materials.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal claims the requested amount did not, at time of submission, breach the applicable 350M Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713, and links to the relevant governance action.
missing: Independent evidence for: Mithril is described as using stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way so nodes and applications can verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says Mithril addresses slow full-node synchronization and centralized-trust dependence by improving secure access to and verification of blockchain state.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states that funding Mithril through Intersect MBO supports Cardano scaling while preserving decentralization guarantees and reducing dependency on centralized indexers and hosted APIs.

Independent evidence for: The document states that Teragone has not previously received direct funding from Cardano treasury or community governance programs, but has contributed to Mithril for several years through IOG-funded collaboration.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 3,810,423 ada, consisting of 3,699,440 ada for Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ada for an Intersect Budget Administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Mithril is described as using stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way so nodes and applications can verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
